bh.shot_strategy: On this 271ft hole, an 8 o'clock crosswind from the right will push your LHBH towards the left; a hyzer flip with a stable putter should be manageable, but requires good control.

fh.shot_strategy: An 8 o'clock crosswind from the right on this 271ft hole demands a controlled LHFH with a stable putter to counter the wind's push to the left.

wind_info.description: Slight 10 o'clock crosswind from the right

bh.shot_strategy: With a slight 10 o'clock crosswind from the right on this 244ft hole, a stable putter thrown with a hyzer will help keep your RHBH on line and prevent it from drifting left.

fh.shot_strategy: For the 244ft Hole C, a slight 10 o'clock crosswind from the right is best handled with a stable putter thrown RHFH for control.

bh.shot_strategy: On this 244ft hole, the slight 10 o'clock crosswind from the right will affect your LHBH; throw a stable putter with a hyzer to keep it on target.

fh.shot_strategy: A controlled LHFH with a stable putter is recommended for the 244ft Hole C, considering the slight 10 o'clock crosswind from the right.

wind_info.description: Moderate 3 o'clock crosswind from the left

bh.shot_strategy: With a moderate 3 o'clock crosswind from the left on this 202ft hole, an understable fairway driver thrown on a hyzer flip will help your RHBH fight the wind and finish straight.

fh.shot_strategy: For the 202ft Hole D, a moderate 3 o'clock crosswind from the left is ideal for an understable fairway driver thrown RHFH with a flex line.

bh.shot_strategy: On this 202ft hole, a moderate 3 o'clock crosswind from the left can be managed with a stable putter thrown flat or with a slight hyzer for your LHBH.

fh.shot_strategy: A controlled LHFH with a stable putter is recommended for the 202ft Hole D, to counteract the moderate 3 o'clock crosswind from the left.

wind_info.description: Direct 12 o'clock headwind

bh.shot_strategy: With a direct 12 o'clock headwind on this 265ft hole, use an overstable fairway driver thrown flat or with a slight hyzer for a predictable flight path.

fh.shot_strategy: A 12 o'clock headwind on this 265ft hole demands an overstable fairway driver, thrown with a clean RHFH for maximum stability and distance.

bh.shot_strategy: For the 265ft Hole E, a direct 12 o'clock headwind requires an overstable disc for your LHBH; throw with a controlled hyzer for best results.

fh.shot_strategy: To combat the 12 o'clock headwind on this 265ft hole, an overstable fairway driver thrown with a firm LHFH will provide the stability needed.
